name: Pandora's Jar
description: Pandora's Jar, often mistakenly referred to as Pandora's Box, is a pivotal artifact in Greek mythology. Crafted by Hephaestus on the orders of Zeus, the jar was a gift to Pandora, the first woman. Filled with all the evils known to humankind – diseases, famine, war, sorrow, and countless other afflictions – it was intended as a means of punishing humanity for Prometheus's theft of fire. Zeus, angered by Prometheus's defiance, sought to inflict a lasting curse upon mortals. Pandora was explicitly warned never to open the jar, but driven by curiosity, she eventually succumbed to temptation. Upon opening it, all the evils escaped and spread across the world, plaguing humanity forever. Only one thing remained trapped inside: Elpis, usually translated as Hope. The interpretation of Hope's presence varies; some see it as a consolation, a means of enduring the hardships released, while others view it as another evil, a deceptive illusion that prolongs suffering. The jar serves as a cautionary tale about the dangers of curiosity, disobedience, and the inevitability of suffering in human existence. Its legacy continues to resonate in modern culture, symbolizing the unleashing of unforeseen consequences and the enduring power of hope in the face of adversity. The exact nature of the evils and the specific implications of Hope's entrapment are subjects of ongoing debate and interpretation within the study of Greek mythology.
subtitle: Releasing evils upon the world.
origin: Crafted by Hephaestus on Zeus's orders.
currentLocation: Lost to time; symbolic representation in stories

keyMyths.title: The Creation of Pandora
keyMyths.description: Zeus orders Hephaestus to create Pandora as punishment for Prometheus's theft of fire. She is given a jar filled with evils and warned not to open it.

keyMyths.title: The Opening of the Jar
keyMyths.description: Driven by curiosity, Pandora opens the jar, releasing all the evils into the world. Only Hope remains trapped inside.

sources.author: Hesiod
sources.description: A didactic poem detailing the myth of Pandora and her jar.
sources.text: A didactic poem detailing the myth of Pandora and her jar.
